steps.title: Find the shared steroid claim
steps.content: Text 1 says bison and elk samples had similar levels of beta-sitosterol and stigmastanol. Text 2 says fecal-steroidal profiles alone are not sufficient to distinguish bison from elk, which also points to similarity between their profiles.

steps.title: Use only both-text support
steps.content: B claims every ungulate species has a unique profile, contradicting the bison-elk similarity. C discusses preservation over long time spans, which neither text establishes. D makes a prevalence claim about bison and elk versus moose that is not supported by both texts. The remaining conclusion follows from both passages. The answer is A.
